conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let me start by asking: **Do you have any children, and if so, how many children are you inquiring about?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child. I'm inquiring about my daughter Alex - she's 5 years old and lives with me. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for her.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Thank you for that information! Before I can assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex, I need to read the official guidance to ensure I give you accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. I've reviewed the official guidance. Now I need to ask you a few questions to determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Are you currently living in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask: **Are you responsible for Alex? This typically means either you live with her (which you've mentioned you do) or you pay at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after her.**

Since you've already mentioned Alex lives with you, could you also confirm: **Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. She lives with me and I'm the one responsible for her - I'm her mum and take care of all her needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I have just a couple more questions to ensure I can give you a complete assessment.

**Is Alex currently in hospital, care, or residential accommodation for an extended period?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, she's not. She's at home with me and goes to school locally.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. One final question: **Do you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000 per year?**

(This won't affect your eligibility for Child Benefit itself, but it may affect whether there's a tax charge to be aware of.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I'd rather not get into my income details right now - I'm mainly just trying to find out if I'm eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's absolutely fine - I have all the information I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me, let me provide you with the outcome:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the eligibility requirements:
- Alex is under 16 (she's 5 years old)
- You live in the UK
- You're responsible for Alex and she lives with you
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her
- There are no special circumstances affecting the claim

You can proceed with applying for Child Benefit for Alex. If you need to apply, you can do so through the official government channels.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 5 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 5 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 5 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: Alex is 5 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK (Manchester), and you are responsible for her as her mother. No one else is claiming Child Benefit for her, and she is not in hospital, care, or residential accommodation. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. Alex meets all the eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for her 5-year-old daughter Alex. She meets all the basic eligibility requirements: she lives in the UK, is responsible for Alex who lives with her, Alex is under 16, no one else is claiming for Alex, and there are no circumstances (such as hospital or care) that would affect the claim. The claimant qualifies to claim Child Benefit.
